creator: exploringlondon
caption:
ad CHEAP DAYS OUT IN THE UK - PART 1 - LEEDS! We love this city, there’s so much to see, the food is great and here’s an itinerary that will save you money too!

Book a train direct with @lner up to 2 weeks in advance for the cheapest rate and use your railcard for 30% off! There’s lots of free things to do and see in Leeds as well as the amazing Mondego ceramic outlet which is so affordable too! Ending with a Thai gem where you can get a starter and a main for under £12, what a BARGAIN!

Book your ticket to Leeds now, save this itinerary for your trip and follow for part 2 - which city should we visit next?!

transcription.text: Welcome to our new series Cheap Days Out in the UK where each week we'll be going to a new location on a budget trying to save as much money as we can and first up we're going to Leeds to get their Webook to train direct with LNER two weeks in advance to get a cheaper rate and you can save up to 30% using a rail card. Get off at Leeds station and walk to Mundago Outlet. They sell incredible Portuguese ceramics by weight so it is so affordable and you can find so much in here and the interiors are also beautiful. Just outside you'll find a path where you can walk down the canal which is beautiful and you'll end up at Bake, an amazing bakery with delicious sweet treats, focaccia and great coffee. Then head to the city centre where Leeds Art Gallery, the Central Library and Leeds City Museum are all free to see. For lunch head to Whitecloth Hall which is a food hall in a historic building and make sure you visit Kirkgate Market, one of the largest indoor markets in Europe and also the site of the first M&S. Grab some dinner before your train home at the incredible Mummy Tire where you can get a starter and a main for under £12 and if you are an LNER Perks member you can check the app to see what offers are available. Save this video for your next cheap day out in the UK and follow for part two.
